CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-03-2019 Set For Trial On Jul-20-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Chantal Lafrades, Esq. 415-543-4800 of RMKB for Defendant Brady Construction.
Ralph Rhoades, Esq. 415-258-9700 of Parton & Sell for CROSS DEFENDANT AMBER FLOORING, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT AMBER FLOORING, INC.'s DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Amber Flooring, Inc.'s demurrer to the cross-complaint of Brady Construction, Inc. and the Michael parties is sustained without leave to amend. Labor Code 3864 provides that: "If an action as provided in this chapter prosecuted by the employee, the employer, or both jointly against the third person results in judgment against such third person, or settlement by such third person, the employer shall have no liability to reimburse or hold such third person harmless on such judgment or settlement in absence of a written agreement so to do executed prior to the injury." This provision generally bars all personal injury and equitable indemnity claims against the employer of the injured employee. (See Alameda Tank Company, Inc. v. Starkist Foods, Inc. (1980) 103 Cal.App.3d 428, 433 ["The statutory right of the employer, in situations such as are herein involved, to be free from liability beyond that imposed by the worker's compensation laws cannot be evaded by the ingenuity of counsel in presenting his claim for reimbursement under any variety of theories or nomenclature."].) Brady's assertion that it can plead a claim for express indemnity based on its "usual business relationship" with Amber lacks merit. Brady cannot plead a claim for express indemnity unless it is able to show that prior to the alleged injury, it entered into an express indemnity agreement with respect to the property where the injury occurred. The contract that is (improperly) attached to the opposition was executed after Mr. Jimenez's accident and pertains to a different property.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
